step.title: Scaffold & Configure
step.goal:
Create the agent directory and write agent.yaml + frontmatter.md (plus persona.md for both modes; menu.md for primary; instructions.md for subagent — those are filled in Step 4). The vera-cli native format keeps agent.yaml minimal (artifact descriptor only) and puts frontmatter content in frontmatter.md as raw markdown between `---` markers.
If Step 2 identified missing project infrastructure (shared/* files referenced in agent.yaml that don't exist in the project), this step copies the bundled references from agent-creator's references/shared-fragments/ and references/baselines/ to the project's .vera/shared/ location BEFORE the new agent's files are written.

substep.title: Materialize Missing Project Infrastructure
Gather Identity
substep.ask: Let's define the agent's identity: 1. agent_id — lowercase, hyphens (e.g., "code-reviewer"). Used as the directory name and the OpenCode agent ID. 2. character_name — human name for personality (e.g., "Percy", "Tessa", "Cole"). 3. agent_title — formal role (e.g., "Business Analyst", "Web Research Specialist"). 4. emoji — single emoji representing this agent (e.g., "📐", "🔨", "⚒️").
substep.note: For subagents: character_name + agent_title + emoji are not surfaced through the agent-activation block (subagents don't include it). Set them anyway for documentation symmetry, but they won't appear in the compiled artifact.

substep.title: Write Description
substep.ask: What's the one-paragraph description? (becomes the `description:` field in frontmatter.md) - For primary agents: what appears in the agent picker and steers model attention when this agent is selected - For subagents: how the orchestrator finds and chooses it (include input shape, output shape, scope — be searchable)

substep.title: Configure Permission Map
substep.action:
Configure the OpenCode permission map for frontmatter.md. Apply principle of least privilege:
Primary agents (typical): skill: {each-skill-from-step-1}: allow read: allow edit: allow bash: allow grep: allow glob: allow list: allow lsp: deny question: allow todowrite: allow todoread: allow task: deny # allow only if the agent dispatches subagents webfetch: deny # research uses web-search-researcher subagent instead
Subagents (typical): read: allow grep: allow glob: allow list: allow edit: deny # subagents observe and report; orchestrator edits write: deny bash: deny # except where structurally required (e.g., validator # runs fitness functions) lsp: deny question: deny # subagents are non-interactive; orchestrator prompts user todowrite: allow # for subagent's own internal tracking todoread: allow task: deny # subagents don't spawn further subagents (Lock 10 worker # is the documented exception — opt in explicitly) webfetch: deny

substep.title: Pick Color
substep.action:
Pick a hex color for OpenCode's UI. Cross-check against existing agents in vera.manifest.yaml to avoid collisions.
Existing dubstack colors: - Ticketsmith primary: #2E8B57 (green — growth, structure) - Codesmith primary: #B22222 (red — forge fire, urgency) - Prompter primary: #A420D0 (purple — precision, craft) - Ticket-reviewer: #8B4513 (saddle brown — read-only critic) - Codesmith-worker: #C75B12 (orange-brown — apprentice at the forge) - Codesmith-validator: #8B0000 (dark red — verification gate) - Lead-researcher: #4682B4 (steel blue — structured executor)

substep.title: Create Directory and Write agent.yaml
substep.action:
Create the directory: .vera/agents/{agent_id}/
Write agent.yaml using the new dubstack format (NOT the legacy nested-frontmatter format):
```yaml type: agent id: {agent_id} file_name: {agent_id}.md variables: agent_id: {agent_id} character_name: {character_name} agent_title: {agent_title} emoji: "{emoji}" includes: - frontmatter.md - shared/activation.md - shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/agent-activation.md - persona.md - menu.md - shared/agent-close.md ```
For subagents: ```yaml type: agent id: {agent_id} file_name: {agent_id}.md variables: agent_id: {agent_id} includes: - frontmatter.md - shared/baseline/context-md-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/baseline/thoughts-cli-awareness.md # if applicable - shared/subagent-open.md - persona.md - instructions.md - shared/subagent-close.md ``` The variables block supplies {{agent_id}} for subagent-open.md's `` substitution.
Reference: references/templates/agent.template.yaml for the canonical structure with explanatory comments.

substep.title: Write frontmatter.md
substep.action:
Write frontmatter.md as RAW markdown content with the YAML frontmatter between `---` markers. This file is concatenated AS-IS to the top of the compiled artifact, providing the OpenCode YAML frontmatter.
Primary agent template: ```markdown --- mode: primary color: "{hex_color}" permission: skill: {skill-1}: allow {skill-2}: allow read: allow edit: allow bash: allow grep: allow glob: allow list: allow lsp: deny question: allow todowrite: allow todoread: allow task: deny webfetch: deny description: "{one-paragraph description}" --- ```
Subagent template: ```markdown --- mode: subagent color: "{hex_color}" permission: read: allow grep: allow glob: allow list: allow edit: deny bash: deny lsp: deny question: deny todowrite: allow todoread: allow task: deny webfetch: deny description: "{one-paragraph description with input/output contract}" --- ```
Do NOT nest the frontmatter inside agent.yaml — that's the legacy vera-cli format. The dubstack-native format puts frontmatter in frontmatter.md as raw content.

substep.title: Confirm Scaffold
substep.template-output:
Present the written files for review: - .vera/agents/{agent_id}/agent.yaml - .vera/agents/{agent_id}/frontmatter.md - Any project shared/ files copied from bundled references (if {{missing_infrastructure}} was non-empty in Step 2)
Key checks: - agent.yaml has top-level type/id/file_name/variables/includes (no nested frontmatter mapping) - agent.yaml includes match the canonical order from Step 2 - frontmatter.md starts and ends with `---` markers - Permission map follows least-privilege - Variables block has all four identity fields (primary only — subagents skip) - Project shared/ files exist for every shared/* reference in agent.yaml includes
Note: persona.md and (for primaries) menu.md and (for subagents) instructions.md are NOT yet created — those are filled in Step 4.
[a] Adjust [c] Continue [y] YOLO

rules.rule: Start from deny-all, justify each allow
webfetch: deny on primary agents — dispatch web-search-researcher subagent instead (preserves the user-approval gate)
task: deny by default — flip to allow only if the agent dispatches subagents via the Task tool
Subagents: question: deny (orchestrator handles user interaction); edit/bash typically deny unless structurally required
Skill permissions: list each skill from Step 1 with value `allow` (deny here would mean the agent can't invoke the skill even if it's in the menu)
Blue (#4A90D9 / #4682B4): Data, trust, analysis, structured executors
Saddle brown (#8B4513): Read-only critic, conservative reviewer
Dark red (#8B0000): Verification gate, critical halt authority
Short but descriptive: codesmith, ticket-reviewer, codesmith-validator
Must be unique across all agents in dubstack (check .vera/agents/ tree)
Becomes the directory name (.vera/agents/{agent_id}/) AND the compiled file basename ({agent_id}.md)
